channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Exploring Love, Trauma, and Healing in ‘Just For The Summer’
item.description: The novel 'Just For The Summer' by Abby Jimenez is much more than ordinary romance story. Eya, the story follows Justin and Emma, two people living far apart but connected online by a strange pattern ...

item.title: Rivers LGSC Chairman Unveils Legal Library to Improve Local Government Services
item.description: The Chairman of the Rivers State Local Government Service Commission (LGSC), Sir Israel N. Amadi, don yan say him dey committed to put the commission on strong ground. Him talk say through reforms and...

item.title: Global Temperatures Approach Paris Agreement Limit Amid Extreme Weather Events
item.description: February 2026 was one of the warmest on record, with global temperatures reaching 1.49°C above pre-industrial levels, just 0.01°C shy of the 1.5°C target set by the 2015 Paris Agreement. The Copernicu...

item.title: Australia updates travel advisory for the Philippines due to terrorism concerns
item.description: Australia has updated its travel advisory for the Philippines, citing potential risks of terrorism due to escalating tensions in the Middle East. The advisory references a prior warning from the US Em...

item.title: Preparing for Sudden Advances in AI: Implications for Markets, Governance and Nations
item.description: Artificial intelligence is not a technology that improves in a straight line. Instead, it advances in jumps, with periods of quiet stability followed by sudden, disruptive leaps that can upend markets...

item.title: Researchers Develop Light-Controlled Protein Evolution for Dynamic Cellular Functions
item.description: Scientists at the Ecole Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne have developed a novel method called 'optovolution' that uses light to guide the evolution of proteins with dynamic behaviors. Unlike traditi...

item.title: Supreme Court directs tribunal of ex-HC judges to hear appeals against voter exclusions in West Bengal
item.description: The Supreme Court of India on Tuesday ordered the creation of an appellate tribunal consisting of former High Court chief justices and judges to hear appeals related to exclusions from the electoral r...

item.title: Pakistan introduces austerity measures to curb fuel consumption amid Middle East tensions
item.description: Pakistan has implemented emergency austerity measures in response to rising global fuel prices and regional instability. Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced that all schools will close for two wee...

item.title: Middle East Tensions Disrupt South African Fruit Exports
item.description: South African farmers and exporters are feeling the pinch as the ongoing conflict in the Middle East disrupts trade routes and shipments. The Cape Chamber of Commerce and Industry reports that several...

item.title: ABC's National Forum struggles in ratings as AUKUS discussions and other media murmurs surface
item.description: This Crikey Tips and Murmurs column, penned by Cochon Truffier on 10 March 2026, covers a mix of media and political snippets. It highlights low viewership for the ABC's new programme National Forum, ...

item.title: Notorious New Brunswick serial killer Allan Legere dies in custody
item.description: Allan Legere, infamously known as the 'Monster of the Miramichi,' has died at the age of 78 while serving his life sentence at Edmonton Institution in Alberta. Legere was convicted of murdering five p...

item.title: Tinubu Assures Nigerians of Victory Over Terrorists, Highlights Economic Progress During Interfaith Iftar
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don assure Nigerians say the country no go ever surrender to terrorists or any group wey wan cause trouble and destroy our peace, unity, and independence. Him talk this one on Mo...

item.title: Rappler Hosts AMA with ICC Counsel on Duterte’s First Year in ICC Detention
item.description: On March 11, 2026, the Philippines marks one year since former president Rodrigo Duterte’s historic arrest and detention at the International Criminal Court (ICC) in The Hague, Netherlands. Duterte fa...

item.title: Snoop Dogg’s Strategic Role in Boosting Swansea City’s Profile
item.description: In July 2025, Snoop Dogg acquired a minority stake in Swansea City, a Championship football club, instantly raising its profile both domestically and internationally. The rapper's visit to the Swansea...

item.title: Remembering Prof Costa Ricky Mahalu: Tanzania’s Scholar and Diplomat
item.description: Tanzania is deeply mourning the passing of Prof Costa Ricky Mahalu, a highly respected academic, diplomat and legal scholar, who died peacefully at Jakaya Kikwete Cardiac Institute in Dar es Salaam at...

item.title: Parliament Committee Says No Large-Scale Evacuation of Pakistanis from Middle East Needed
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Senior officials from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MoFA) briefed the National Assembly Standing Committee on Overseas Pakistanis that there is no immediate need for mass evacuation of P...

item.title: Three Nigerian Military Commanding Officers Killed by Boko Haram and ISWAP in Borno State
item.description: Naija, for the past one week, Borno State don witness heavy wahala as Boko Haram and ISWAP terrorists don claim the lives of three senior military officers wey dey command forward operations bases. Th...

item.title: Ruto Proposes Contributory Pension Scheme for County Leaders
item.description: President William Ruto on Tuesday called on both UDA and ODM lawmakers to craft legislation that will set up a contributory pension scheme for Governors and Members of County Assemblies (MCAs). Speaki...

item.title: Rappler invites women to share experiences of everyday misogyny in upcoming community chat amid recent congressional sexism controversy
item.description: The article announces a community discussion event amid ongoing sexism in the Philippines. It highlights irony that misogyny persists as Women's Month begins, spotlighting recent controversy where Que...

item.title: Southern Ontario braces for heavy rain and potential flooding
item.description: Southern Ontario is facing a period of heavy rainfall that has prompted authorities to raise concerns about possible flooding in several communities, particularly within the Grand River watershed. Res...
